feed.title: SAP Community - SAP S/4HANA migration cockpit
feed.subtitle: SAP S/4HANA migration cockpit Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Migration Cockpit internal numbering
entry.content:
Hello all, 

 

I am currently working on a migration project with the migration cockpit. What the customer has right now is internal numbering for all materials on a different system. What he needs is that we migrate the materials to S/4 with different internal numbering. I managed to find out that i can change the numbering from external to internal through the control parameters in the migration cockpit. Still it doesn't work out for me. Anyone has a clue how to fix this?

entry.title: Best Practices for Data Migration in a Carve-Out from S/4HANA On-Prem to S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community, 

I’m currently working with a customer who is performing a carve-out, moving from their legacy S/4HANA On-Premise system to S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition. As part of this project, they are facing challenges in identifying the relevant data tables for migration and pinpointing where exactly the necessary data resides in their existing system. I’d like to reach out to this community for any best practices or lessons learned from similar carve-out scenarios.

  • The new system will be based on S/4HANA Cloud, public edition, so we are focusing on a clean and efficient transition.
  • The legacy system is S/4HANA On-Premise, with years of data accumulated. The customer is unsure about the best approach to identify and extract only the necessary data for migration. 

Challenges:


  1. 1. Identifying relevant tables: The customer is unsure about which tables to migrate and how to locate the key tables in their On-Premise system.
    2. Data relevance: There’s some confusion around how to extract **only the relevant data**, without transferring unnecessary historical data.
    3. Table mapping for migration tools: Aligning On-Prem tables with SAP Migration Cockpit templates and the standard migration objects available for the Cloud edition has also proven to be challenging.

Key Questions:

  • What best practices exist for identifying and mapping the critical tables in S/4HANA On-Prem for a carve-out scenario?
  • Are there any efficient methodologies or tools that can help pinpoint relevant data and ensure it aligns with the S/4HANA Cloud public edition data structure?
  • How do you ensure the customer extracts only the required data, avoiding clutter or excessive historical records? 

I’d appreciate any advice, strategies, or best practices that you have found effective in your own experience—particularly in scenarios where S/4HANA On-Prem is being transitioned to the public Cloud edition. We want to ensure that our data migration is as clean and efficient as possible.

Thanks in advance for your input!

Best regards,
Yannick

entry.title: Transport a project between clients in Migration Cookpit
entry.content:
We need a help, to solve a problem.
We need you tell us the steps, exactly, how to transport a project between clients in Migration Cookpit. 
We need transport the project the client DH0-650  to DH0-550 and DH0-110. 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Customer Data Migration - From General to Sales and Company
entry.content:
Hello All,
I would like to understand if it is possible to "integrate" already uploaded General Data, where Public Cloud gave back customer progressive number, with Company and Sales Data.

Does it exist a dedicated migration project or App? 

Thanks
S

entry.title: Migration Cockpit Object custom modifications review before update in production system
entry.content:
Hi,

Trying to migrate data using Migration Cockpit staging table approach. We have had issues with objects containing custom fields. We added custom fields in development system and transported them to quality and then production. Objects containing custom fields have had short dump in quality LTMOM, in migration cockpit fiori app message "upload canceled; field definitions of upload file x.xml are out of date" or "column sterilization failed". Fields seem to be right looking at the tables. Now new project has been made, transported to production. In production fiori objects containing custom fields are giving error "This migration object contains custom modifications that must be reviewed before the update can be applied. In order to proceed, execute the following option from the toolbar of SAP S/4HANA Migration Object Modeler: Update Object.". But this can not be performed in production system object modeler. Is there a work around for this and how to proceed? The transport of copies doesn't seem to be option...

Thanks

entry.title: Transport of Migration Project from one environment to another environment.
entry.content:
How to transfert/transport Migration Project from Dev to Quality System? 



------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: LTMC Projects are not reflecting under SAP PACKAGE (SE80)
entry.content: I have created Package to move LTMC project from one environment to another environment. When I have created new project in Migrate Your Data application, I choose that particular package in the selection but when I see the Package details in SE80 I cannot see those added Project in the Package. please check the attached image

entry.title: Migrate Full Set of Sales Partners
entry.content:
Hi,

We are implementing SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, and migrating from a non-SAP ERP.

Due to the configuration the consultants designed, each Ship-To address must be created as a business partner. This means there is a  Main BP, and a Ship-To BP.  So in the Main BP's Sales Area the 'Ship-To' Partner Function  is assigned to the Ship-To BP; and reversely, in the Ship-To BP's Sales Area, the 'Sold-To' Partner Function is assigned to the Main BP.

In other words, in the Main BP's Sales Area the 'Ship-To' Partner Function  is assigned to the Ship-To BP; and reversely, in the Ship-To BP's Sales Area, the 'Sold-To' Partner Function is assigned to the Main BP.

The problem appears when I simulate a full set of BPs, including the Main and ShipTo BP types. No matter what combination of 'Partner Function' (PARVW) and 'Customer ID' (KUNN2) I use in the Sales Partner staging table (S_CUST_SALES_PARTNER), the system seems unable to recognize the 'Customer ID' even if it is included in the same batch of simulated BPs.

To clarify with an example, this is what my table would look like

Name (just for the example)Number (KUNNR)     Partner Function (PARVW)Customer Id (KUNN2)
MAIN001AG (Sold-To)001
MAIN001WE (Ship-To)002
SHIPTO002AG (Sold-To)001
SHIPTO002WE (Ship-To)002

The error messages I would get would be:

Messages for Source Record: 001
Entry 002 does not exist in KNA1 (check entry)
KNA1-KUNN2: Invalid value (foreign key check failed)
Customer: Plausibility check failed
Customer 002 has not been created for sales area 2710 10 00

Of course, I don't get errors if I migrate Main BPs first (without reference to other BPs), and then I migrate Ship-To BPs using the migrated BP's Customer Number (automatically assigned) as 'Customer ID'. But this approach will only bring me problems.

Am I missing anything out to be able to migrate all BPs at once?

Thanks a lot!

 

EDIT: I wronlgly thought the 'Partner Description' could help me, but no

.

entry.title: Is job scheduling possible in Migration Cockpit? How?
Migration Cockpit's Staging table: Remote DB connection auth methods
entry.content:
What kind of authentication is supported by the remote DB connection schema from Migration Cockpit?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA migration cockpit: Accounting data creation with price control V and price determinatio
entry.content:
Hi experts!

 

In Migration Cockpit (Fiori version), I have the next message " Price Control V is not allowed to Price Control Determination 3" while uploading finished goods.

However, if I am going to create the accounting data in MM01, then I have no problem.

On the other hand, if I try to create the finished product with price control S in Migration Cockpit, I get the following error: Only price control “V” for separate valuation is possible.

So, I'm quite stuck with this issue.

Can someone give me a hand?

entry.title: Unable to specify the RFC system in migration cockpit direct transfer
entry.content: When using the Migration Cockpit - Direct Transfer approach, i am unable to specify the source ECC system.  This happens after a client copy is done and in the new client.  Is there anywhere this could be specified or is there a process around this?  Creating a new project everytime, so that it allows us to specify the source system doesn't seems to be an ideal practice.

entry.title: Data Migration with forground Processing
entry.content:
How to perform Data Migration with foreground Processing? 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Migration Cockpit: Error: Instance XXXX skipped to avoid multiple updates
entry.content:
Dear Expert,

I am trying to extend material master to another plant and storage location using Migration Cockpit, I use Migration Object "Product - extend existing record by new org levels",

I fill in  Basic Data Sheet (Product Number, Run ID), Plant Data Sheet (Product Number, Run ID, Plant), Storage Location Sheet (Product Number, Run ID, Plant, Storage Location).

For Upload to Mapping Tasks steps are suceed, but for Simulate Steps, I got error message like this :

Migration Cockpit: Error: Instance XXXX skipped to avoid multiple updates

This issue already mentioned in SAP Note 3356771, is this migration object can not be used to upload case like I mentioned above?

entry.title: Are you able to extend multiple vendors and assign the same purchasing organisation by mass change?
entry.content:
Hello

I have a list of vendors I want to extend to use a purchasing organisation XX01

Is this something that can be done on mass I believe I have to

1. Extend the vendor to use Purchase Organisation (our role FLVN01)

2. Associate it to Purchase Organisation XX01, and assign the required static data

If this is something possible are there any blogs to achieve this?

Thanks

entry.title: Internal table names for data of direct transfer inside migration cockpit
entry.content:
Hi,

I'm looking for the internal table names where the data - read from the source system - is stored in S/4 for simulation and migration . Up to now I've only found the table names for the instances (starting with /1LT/PE*), but they only contain the key fields and not the data fields of the source tables, e.g. MARA, MARC, MBEW for object 'Product'.

entry.title: What data needs to be filled in Data controller tab of Customer/Supplier LTMC template?
entry.content: I started migration of Customer master, and I came across data controller tab in the Customer Master LTMC template which was not there in the earlier version. What data should be there in that particular tab?

entry.title: SAP S4HANA migration project and custom code analysis project
entry.content:
Hi SAP

 

What is the different between creating SAP S4HANA migration project and custom code analysis project.

 

I could see the same custom code analysis in both except, selecting the variant while creating custom code analysis project.

 

Regards,


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Automatic assignment of decimals zeros for specific fields in QM - Inspection plan Migration Object
entry.content:
When migrating quality inspection plans from SAP EECC to SAP S/4HANA, the decimal values in the numeric fields of the quantitative inspection characteristics are not preserved. During the migration of the inspection plan, the value 10 is automatically assigned in the decimal field (STELLEN) for each characteristic, and 7 zeros are added to the right of the numeric values (SOLLWERT, TOLERANZUN, TOLERANZOB).

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Link sales orders to contracts in bulk for Data Migration
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have query regarding data migration. Actually, during Sales order migration, we are not able to link the sales order contract number in the sales order. There is no field for the contract in the template of the sale order. We have uploaded the Sales order via migration cockpit. In order to, link the Contract number in the Sales Order we have planned to use LSMW but unfortunately it is not working properly. 

It will be very hard to do this activity manually from VA02 since the volume is high. 

So, can we add the sales contract of a sales order in bulk?

Thank you.
